2605.06919.title: Can LLMs Take Retrieved Information with a Grain of Salt?
2605.06919.paper_summary: Large language models have demonstrated impressive retrieval-augmented capabilities. However, a crucial area remains underexplored: their ability to appropriately adapt responses to the certainty of the retrieved information. It is a limitation with real consequences in high-stakes domains like medicine and finance. We evaluate eight LLMs on their context-certainty obedience, measuring how well they adjust responses to match expressed context certainty. Our analysis reveals systematic limitations: LLMs struggle to recall prior knowledge after observing an uncertain context, misinterpret expressed certainties, and overtrust complex contexts. To address these, we propose an interaction strategy combining prior reminders, certainty recalibration, and context simplification. This approach reduces obedience errors by 25% on average, without modifying model weights, demonstrating the efficacy of interaction design in enhancing LLM reliability. Our contributions include a principled evaluation metric, empirical insights into LLMs' uncertainty handling, and a portable strategy to improve context-certainty obedience across diverse LLMs.
2605.06919.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型展示了令人印象深刻的檢索增強能力。<lb/>然而，有一個關鍵領域仍未被充分探索：它們根據檢索信息的確定性適當調整回應的能力。<lb/>這在醫療和金融等高風險領域是一個具有實際後果的限制。<lb/>我們評估了八個大型語言模型在其上下文確定性遵從性方面的表現，測量它們如何調整回應以匹配表達的上下文確定性。<lb/>我們的分析揭示了系統性限制：大型語言模型在觀察到不確定的上下文後難以回憶先前的知識，誤解表達的確定性，並對複雜的上下文過度信任。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了一種結合先前提醒、確定性重新校準和上下文簡化的互動策略。<lb/>這種方法平均減少了25%的遵從性錯誤，而不改變模型權重，展示了互動設計在增強大型語言模型可靠性方面的有效性。<lb/>我們的貢獻包括一個原則性的評估指標、對大型語言模型不確定性處理的實證洞察，以及一種可攜帶的策略，以改善不同大型語言模型的上下文確定性遵從性。
2605.06919.author: Behzad Shayegh et.al.
2605.06919.authors: Behzad Shayegh, Mohamed Osama Ahmed, Fred Tung, Leo Feng
